purpose: User-friendly reading and practical exercises for people trying to reduce unwanted pornography use.
display_rule: Show a short in-app summary and one practical action first. The external link should be optional further reading.

resources.title: When Sexual Behaviour Feels Out of Control
resources.description: Explains how sexual behaviour can become difficult to control, what warning signs to notice and how it may affect work, relationships and emotional wellbeing.
resources.user_takeaway: The main issue is not simply how often something happens. It is whether you feel unable to control it and whether it is harming your life.
resources.suggested_action: Write down one area of your life that has been negatively affected and one change you would like to make.

resources.title: Ways to Manage Compulsive Sexual Behaviour
resources.description: Introduces practical approaches used to manage urges, recognise unhealthy patterns and make sexual content less private and less easily accessible.
resources.user_takeaway: Recovery involves learning how to respond differently to urges, thoughts, emotions and high-risk situations.
resources.suggested_action: Identify one private or high-risk situation that you can make safer today.

resources.title: What Does Addiction Mean?
resources.description: Explains addiction in simple language as losing control over an activity even though it causes harm.
resources.user_takeaway: A behaviour becomes concerning when control is repeatedly lost and important parts of life are neglected.
resources.suggested_action: Ask yourself: Do I still freely choose this behaviour, or do I often feel pushed into it?

resources.title: Ride Out an Urge
resources.description: Teaches you to observe an urge like a wave that rises, reaches a peak and eventually passes instead of immediately acting on it.
resources.user_takeaway: An urge is uncomfortable, but it is temporary and does not have to control your next action.
resources.suggested_action: Set a three-minute timer. Notice where the urge appears in your body and observe how it changes.

resources.title: Understand What Triggered the Urge
resources.description: Shows how to record what happened before an urge, how strong it felt and how you responded.
resources.user_takeaway: Repeated urges often follow recognisable situations, feelings, thoughts or routines.
resources.suggested_action: Record the time, place, emotion, urge intensity and what you did next.

resources.title: Learn to Stay With Difficult Feelings
resources.description: Explains why escaping difficult emotions can strengthen unhealthy patterns and teaches ways to accept and manage temporary discomfort.
resources.user_takeaway: An uncomfortable feeling is not an emergency. You can experience it without immediately escaping through pornography.
resources.suggested_action: Name your current emotion and choose one calming or constructive activity for the next ten minutes.

resources.title: Respond to Yourself Without Shame
resources.description: Teaches how to replace harsh self-criticism with a calmer and more constructive response to mistakes.
resources.user_takeaway: Taking responsibility does not require insulting or hating yourself. Shame can make another lapse more likely.
resources.suggested_action: Write what happened, what you learned and what you will change next time without describing yourself negatively.

resources.title: Break the Procrastination Cycle
resources.description: Explains why people avoid uncomfortable tasks and provides strategies for beginning work instead of escaping into distracting behaviour.
resources.user_takeaway: Pornography may sometimes be an escape from boredom, pressure or a difficult task rather than the original problem.
resources.suggested_action: Choose one avoided task and work on it for only five minutes.

resources.title: You Are More Than Your Behaviour
resources.description: Explains how negative beliefs about yourself develop and how to replace global judgments with more balanced beliefs.
resources.user_takeaway: Doing something you regret does not mean that your entire identity is bad, hopeless or permanently damaged.
resources.suggested_action: Write three qualities, roles or actions that describe you apart from pornography use.

resources.title: Create a Safer Nighttime Routine
resources.description: Provides practical information about sleep habits, insomnia and creating healthier evening routines.
resources.user_takeaway: Late-night tiredness, privacy and unrestricted device access can create a high-risk combination.
resources.suggested_action: Choose a device cutoff time and charge your phone outside the bedroom tonight.

resources.title: Take Care of Your Mental Health
resources.description: Offers practical guidance on sleep, exercise, relaxation, priorities, gratitude and maintaining contact with supportive people.
resources.user_takeaway: Recovery becomes easier when your whole life is more stable, not only when pornography is blocked.
resources.suggested_action: Choose one basic need to improve today: sleep, food, movement, relaxation or connection.

resources.title: Reconnect With Other People
resources.source: Office of the U.S. Surgeon General
resources.description: Explains why meaningful human connection matters and provides ideas for strengthening relationships and community involvement.
resources.user_takeaway: Isolation can make unhealthy coping behaviours more attractive. Recovery should include real human connection.
resources.suggested_action: Send one honest message to a trusted person or make a plan to meet someone offline.

resources.title: Use Physical Activity to Reset
resources.source: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
resources.description: Explains the immediate and long-term health benefits of physical activity, including benefits for sleep, mood and thinking.
resources.user_takeaway: Movement can be a useful healthy action when you need to leave a triggering environment or interrupt an automatic routine.
resources.suggested_action: Leave your device behind and walk outside for ten minutes.

resources.title: How Serious Is What I Am Experiencing?
resources.description: Helps readers consider how strongly emotional or behavioural difficulties are affecting everyday life and when additional support may be appropriate.
resources.user_takeaway: Self-help can be useful, but severe or worsening problems should not be handled by an app alone.
resources.suggested_action: Consider whether the problem is affecting your safety, work, studies, relationships or ability to function normally.
